summary: Hotz argues that a 'professional managerial class' (PMC) dominates modern organizations and society, playing zero-sum political games rather than creating real value. He uses an overheard coffee shop conversation as an exemplar of this mentality—someone focused entirely on internal positioning rather than productive contribution. He contrasts this with comma.ai's culture of dictatorial clarity focused on solving real problems. Hotz believes this parasitic class may comprise 37-73% of society and calls for people to quit or fire PMC members while embracing a growth mindset. He sees crypto and AI as potential tools to defeat this class and restore genuine value creation.
key_points: The 'professional managerial class' plays zero-sum games within organizations, prioritizing personal advancement over creating value
Google grew from 32,467 to 139,995 employees while arguably delivering worse services—questioning what all these people actually do
Dictatorial leadership with clear values is preferable to a power vacuum that 'the worst people will fill'
Wealth is not fixed: historical kings couldn't buy antibiotics or iPhones regardless of their riches
Crypto and AI represent new tools that could change the power dynamics and defeat the PMC
money_quotes: Do not leave a power vacuum, the worst people will fill it. Dictatorship is fine as long as there's free exit.
We grow the pie, exactly how the pie is split is much less important than the overall size of the pie.
The king of France couldn't trade all of France for a single iPhone.
Google in 2011 had 32,467 employees, Google in 2021 has 139,995. I argue most of Google's services are worse today than they were in 2011. What do all these people do?
Unlike Google, which tried to not have politics and ended up infiltrated by these people, comma has an extreme amount of politics, but they are all mine and there's no room for any others.
They are a large, growing, decentralized, parasitic class, and they have a zero sum mentality about the world.
Let them wirehead. But don't ever let them near the levers of power again.
key_insight: Modern organizations are dominated by a professional managerial class playing zero-sum political games, and the solution is to reject their mentality, focus on genuine value creation, and never leave power vacuums for them to fill.
